# terrible_community_vmware_vmware_dvs_portgroup (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `port_binding` (String) The type of port binding determines when ports in a port group are assigned to virtual machines. See VMware KB 1022312 https://kb.vmware.com/s/article/1022312 for more details.
- `portgroup_name` (String) The name of the portgroup that is to be created or deleted.
- `switch_name` (String) The name of the distributed vSwitch the port group should be created on.
- `vlan_id` (String) The VLAN ID that should be configured with the portgroup, use 0 for no VLAN. If `vlan_trunk=true`, this can be a combination of multiple ranges and numbers, example: 1-200, 205, 400-4094. The valid range is from 0 to 4094. Overlapping ranges are allowed. If `vlan_private=true`, the corresponding private VLAN should already be configured in the distributed vSwitch.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `in_traffic_shaping` (String) Dictionary which configures the ingress traffic shaping settings for the portgroup.
- `mac_learning` (String) This option is deprecated in favour of `network_policy.mac_learning` and will be removed in 7.0.0. Dictionary which configures MAC learning for portgroup. Ignored if `network_policy.inherited=true`. Ignored if `network_policy.mac_learning` is defined. Beware that setting this to enabled might disable the inheritance of promiscuous mode and so on!
- `net_flow` (String) Indicate whether or not the virtual machine IP traffic that flows through a vds gets analyzed by sending reports to a NetFlow collector.
- `network_policy` (String) Dictionary which configures the different security values for portgroup.
- `num_ports` (Number) The number of ports the portgroup should contain.
- `out_traffic_shaping` (String) Dictionary which configures the egress traffic shaping settings for the portgroup.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `port_allocation` (String) Elastic port groups automatically increase or decrease the number of ports as needed. Only valid if `port_binding=static`. Will be `elastic` if not specified and `port_binding=static`. Will be `fixed` if not specified and `port_binding=ephemeral`.
- `port_policy` (String) Dictionary which configures the advanced policy settings for the portgroup.
- `portgroup_description` (String) Description of the portgroup
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Determines if the portgroup should be present or not.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `teaming_policy` (String) Dictionary which configures the different teaming values for portgroup.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vlan_private` (Boolean) Indicates whether this is for a private VLAN or not. Mutually exclusive with `vlan_trunk` parameter.
- `vlan_trunk` (Boolean) Indicates whether this is a VLAN trunk or not. Mutually exclusive with `vlan_private` parameter.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
